channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden announces 2 new national monuments in California after wildfires scuttled previous event
item.description: President Joe Biden has announced the creation of two California national monuments that will honor Native American tribes while shielding picturesque mountains and deserts from mining and energy development.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nevada elections leader urges real-time efficiency over changing deadlines
item.description: The influx of mail-in ballots collected on Election Day led to delays in tabulation across Nevada, not the number of ballots received in the days after, Democratic Nevada Secretary of State Cisco Aguilar said ahead of the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hegseth confronts allegations of misconduct as senators grill Trump's choice for Pentagon chief
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump's choice for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, vowed Tuesday to foster a “warrior culture” at the Pentagon and confronted allegations of sexual assault and excessive drinking and questions about his derisive views of women in combat during a heated Senat
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, TARA COPP and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden in late push to boost Indo-Pacific ties sends three pacts to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration is seeking to shore up ties with countries in the Indo-Pacific in its waning days in office by sending to Congress three key agreements that would cement relations with critical allies and partners in a region increasingly dominated by China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Freedom Caucus takes control of the Wyoming House, marking its first chance to lead
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — As President-elect Donald Trump plans bold moves for his first days in office, so too are conservative lawmakers in Wyoming, the first state where Trump-friendly Freedom Caucus members have won control of a statehouse chamber.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gold Star families devastated by Biden's botched Afghanistan withdrawal endorse Hegseth for SecDef
item.description: Gold Star families who lost loved ones during the Biden administration's botched withdrawal from Afghanistan endorsed Pete Hegseth to be the next secretary of Defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CA lawmaker demands federal probe amid fire destruction, as Sacramento seeks unity and accountability
item.description: California State Assemblymembers spoke out on varying aspects of the LA wildfires and emergency preparedness, with one sending letters to Washington for investigatory help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some of the state’s public colleges and universities from admitting students who are in the country without legal permission.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House removes Cuba's state sponsor of terrorism designation, reversing Trump administration move
item.description: The Biden administration lifted Cuba's designation as a state sponsor of terrorism on Tuesday, four years after the Trump White House issued the designation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk poised to occupy White House office space: report
item.description: Elon Musk could occupy space in the Eisenhower Executive Office Building next to the West Wing to head up the newly created Department of Government Efficiency, known as DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker Johnson orders US Capitol flags raised to full height for Trump's inauguration
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson has ordered that flags at the U.S. Capitol be raised to their full height on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP Hegseth holdout presses Defense secretary nominee on her top 3 issues in military
item.description: Initial Republican holdout supporting Pete Hegseth as secretary of Defense, Sen. Joni Ernst, questioned the nominee about her top three issues during his hearing Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California Democrats approve $50M to fight Trump policies
item.description: One lawmaker said they don't have to choose "between helping LA recover from wildfires and protecting CA from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Utah's push to wrest control of public land from the federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court turned back a push by the state of Utah to wrest control of vast areas of public land from the federal government, marking a small victory for land conservation advocates who worry that similar efforts may escalate in a Republican-controlled Washington.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP incumbents brace for MAGA primary challengers
item.description: Several GOP Senate incumbents are already staring down the threat of primary challenges next year as friction builds between the MAGA and establishment wings. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. faces a complicated confirmation with some opposition on both sides of the aisle
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s views on vaccines and abortion rights are raising alarms across the political spectrum, as he's one of the more controversial picks for the president-elect's Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: What Project 2025 says about the first 180 days of a presidency
item.description: Project 2025 lists four pillars as part of its plan, and the fourth pillar is an unpublished 180-day "Transition Playbook.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Incoming Trump team questions civil servants at National Security Council about their loyalty
item.description: Incoming senior Trump officials have begun questioning career civil servants serving on the White House National Security Council about who they voted for, their political contributions in the 2024 election and whether they've made social media posts that could be considered incriminating by the Tru
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis's call for special session 'premature,' Senate President, House Speaker say
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a special legislative session to address illegal immigration, among other issues, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance says Jan. 6 participants who committed violence 'obviously' shouldn't be pardoned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance says people responsible for the violence during the Capitol riot “obviously” should not be pardoned, as President-elect Donald Trump is promising to use his clemency power on behalf of many of those who tried on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Exit interview: DOT Sec. Buttigieg on infrastructure act and the road ahead
item.description: In an exit interview with All Things Considered, DOT Secretary Pete Buttigieg reflects on the Biden administration's infrastructure act and why it didn't resonate with some voters.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow, Avery Keatley, and Adam Raney, MPR News

item.title: Biden awards Pope Francis the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction
item.description: The White House commended Pope Francis for his commitment to serving the poor, as well as advocating for peace and protecting the planet.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: ACLU sues Gov. DeSantis on behalf of Florida voters over special elections
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union is suing Gov. Ron DeSantis for not calling special elections for state House and Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump is sentenced in hush money case
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump received an unconditional discharge for his criminal conviction, meaning he will get a criminal record but no other penalties.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a federal law that could force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
